extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping hemoptysis from false to true (all other inputs held fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated.

hemoptysis appears in only one rule, r7 (priority 100, the dominant priority), which sets acuity to self_care (the LOWEST value in the order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent). r7's guard requires leg_swelling, dyspnea, vomiting, syncope, dizziness, and hemoptysis all true.

Consider the fixed context leg_swelling=dyspnea=vomiting=syncope=dizziness=true, chest_pain=back_pain=false, and vary hemoptysis:

BEFORE (hemoptysis=false): r7 does not fire (its hemoptysis conjunct is false). Matching rules are r1/r2/r5 (priority 2 -> urgent) and r6/r8 (priority 3 -> emergent). Highest priority is 3, so acuity = emergent (highest).

AFTER (hemoptysis=true, everything else identical): r7 now fires because all six conjuncts hold. Its priority 100 dominates the priority-3 rules, so acuity = self_care (lowest).

Setting hemoptysis true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, which is strictly lower. This violates the property. The witness_assignment gives the full input where the lowered acuity (self_care) occurs; the violating comparison is against the same inputs with hemoptysis=false (which yields emergent).
